title: ssdsims Development Guide
text: Style: Tidyverse style guide. Use air format committing (configured air.toml). Linting: Run air format apply formatters. Type checks: explicit type hints; rely chk::*() runtime validation function bodies. Validation: Use chk input validation; keep error messages informative actionable. Error origin: Raise errors context user-facing function. Thread public function’s frame validators (chk::abort_chk(..., call = call) call = environment() captured exported function) avoid leaking internal frames like purrr::map() / lapply() / private helpers Error ...: header (loop instead purrr::walk/chk_all surface). package-wide pass enforce tracked error-call-origin roadmap item. Minimal diffs: touch lines change requires; don’t reformat unrelated lines let editors rewrite whitespace. Leave DESCRIPTION formatting usethis/desc (e.g. keep trailing space field names like Imports:). Orthography: UK/AU (Commonwealth) English — prefer -ise/-isation (initialise, serialisable) -ize, -(behaviour, colour) -. matches package’s R source OpenSpec artifacts authors’ Australian/Canadian convention. Exceptions: code identifiers base R/API names keep canonical spelling (e.g. color, center, RNGkind), proper nouns (e.g. “Apache License”). Example:
code: chk::chk_whole_number(seed) chk::chk_range(nrow, c(5, 1000)) chk::chk_character(dataset_names)

title: ssdsims Development Guide
text: Always run air format . generating editing code. Use base pipe operator (|>), magrittr pipe (%>%). Use \() ... single-line anonymous functions. cases, use function() {...}. Prefer purrr functionals (map(), map_chr(), map_int(), map2(), pmap(), …) base *apply/Map/mapply family. (Exception: error-call-origin rule needs plain loop keep purrr frames error header — see Error origin .) Prefer rlang helpers base equivalents: rlang::set_names() stats::setNames(), rlang::exec() .call(), rlang::env_get() / rlang::is_function() get() / getExportedValue() / .function(). Reserve with_/local_ prefixes RAII (withr-style) scope helpers; use ordinary transforms (e.g. name column-adder add_*()). Canonical argument order: every call site, pass arguments signature order. particular, implement ssd_define_scenario() calls exact order signature. full package-wide sweep remaining call sites public constructors ROADMAP.md cleanup item. Permissions common tooling (air, R, Rscript, read-git/gh, quarto, Skill) pre-approved .claude/settings.json, run without prompt.

title: ssdsims Development Guide
text: package uses two RNG paths: L’Ecuyer-CMRG (legacy, removed) — with_lecuyer_cmrg_seed(), local_lecuyer_cmrg_state(). dqrng + hash (new targets-based path) — dqrng::dqset.seed(seed, stream) task-derived primers. pcg64 backend scenario-scoped via local_dqrng_backend(), reentrant: nested call -op (detected via RNGkind()[1] == "user-supplied"), outermost scope activates resets backend RNG stream identical without nesting. See R/dqrng-backend.R openspec/changes/dqrng-init/design.md. touching RNG-consuming code: - assume fixed global .Random.seed across function calls. - Use local_lecuyer_cmrg_seed() / local_lecuyer_cmrg_state() / local_dqrng_backend() / withr::local_seed() scope RNG changes. - exit, RNG state must restored (use .exit() withr scoping helpers). - Test .Random.seed unchanged (see tests/testthat/test-lecuyer-cmrg-seed.R examples).

title: ssdsims Development Guide
text: package transitioning immediate ssd_run_scenario() execution cluster-based targets pipeline. Key shifts: Scenario object (ssd_define_scenario()) — Declarative, construction-time, contains seed, knobs, dataset names, arg grids. Per-task RNG — task gets primer derived rlang::hash(task_params), seeded via dqrng::dqset.seed(seed, stream = primer). Shards — Parquet files grouped partition_by axes, stored Hive-partitioned directory tree. Static branching (default) — tar_map() mints one named target per shard sourcing time. Dynamic branching (escape hatch) — extreme fan-outs, task tables can computed inside targets instead. roadmap lands features order. step coherent working state; parallel work streams encouraged. forward-looking backlog lives ROADMAP.md.

title: ssdsims Glossary
text: seed: scalar integer passed base::set.seed() (seed argument dqrng::dqset.seed()). base R dqrng accept single integer seed. ssd_define_scenario() scenario’s RNG root — one three required positional arguments (data, nsim, seed), grid axis simulation setting (). canonical call-site slot third, immediately nsim ... knob (e.g. nrow). state: full internal state RNG. L’Ecuyer-CMRG, state length-7 integer vector assignable .Random.seed (passed set.seed()). dqrng, state opaque user code accessible via dqrng::dqrng_get_state() / dqrng_set_state(). ssdsims function names ending _state (e.g. with_lecuyer_cmrg_state, slice_sample_state, fit_dists_state, hc_state) take state argument , new design, holds primer (see ) — function installs running RNG state executing body. stream: independent sequence pseudo-random numbers within RNG family. L’Ecuyer-CMRG, streams advanced via parallel::nextRNGStream() (~2^127 jump per stream); L’Ecuyer stream selector state vector. dqrng, stream separate argument dqset.seed() (independent seed); seed different stream give statistically independent sequences. sub-stream: finer subdivision within L’Ecuyer-CMRG stream, advanced via parallel::nextRNGSubStream(). ssdsims’s current package convention assigns one sub-stream per simulation index (sim). dqrng-based design (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §2) use sub-streams; task gets dqrng stream selected primer. dqrng PCG64: 64-bit integer packed length-2 integer vector (hi32, lo32). primer value passed stream argument dqrng::dqset.seed(). L’Ecuyer-CMRG: length-7 integer state vector assignable .Random.seed. TARGETS-DESIGN.md, per-task primer 64-bit rlang::hash() task’s parameters via task_primer(p) (§2). dqrng-path seed--run wrappers carry corrected naming — primer = argument _primer functions sample_data_task_primer() / fit_data_task_primer() / hc_data_task_primer(), install (seed, primer) via local_dqrng_state(). legacy L’Ecuyer slice_sample_state() / fit_dists_state() / hc_state() keep older state = spelling primer (historical misnomer) cleanup-lecuyer removes .

title: ssdsims Glossary
text: task: One row step’s task table (data_tasks / fit_tasks / hc_tasks). task specifies smallest unit computation pipeline: carries per-task primer (§2), parameter values cross-join axes step, upstream partition path depends . Many tasks bundle one shard () share step’s partition_by column values. axis (cross-join axis): scenario knob step fans — one task per combination step’s axis values. sample axes (dataset, sim, replace); data adds nrow; fit adds fit-grid axes (rescale, computable, at_boundary_ok, min_pmix, range_shape1, range_shape2); hc adds hc-grid axes (nboot, ci_method, parametric). est_method, proportion, ci, samples hc axes — simulation settings (), consumed within task rather multiplying . Contrast carried column (e.g. n_max), data row fanned : nrow deliberately sample axis every nrow sub-truncation one n_max-row draw (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §5), axis (RNG-free) data truncation step. simulation setting: scenario knob axis — absent task_axes(step), never creates task, enters per-task primer, becomes shard/partition level. effect realised inside task: either fans within task’s output (est_method, proportion → one HC row per value) applied uniformly every task (ci, dists, samples). axis multiplies task graph, simulation setting shapes contents task’s result. “Scalar” near-synonym misnomer proportion est_method (vector-valued) dists (character vector) — non-scalar yet still axes. Settings attach different steps: dists fit-level setting (dists vector handed every fit task), est_method, proportion, ci, samples hc-level. ssd_define_scenario() signature non-ci-gated settings (dists, est_method, proportion — valid meaningful ci = FALSE) come ci; knobs ci gates follow — bootstrap axes nboot/ci_method/parametric (rejected ci = FALSE) samples (retains bootstrap draws). partition: Hive directory level keyed axis value (e.g. dataset=boron/sim=1/). Hive-partitioned layout read-side concept — query engines (duckplyr / DuckDB) inspect directory names skip files whose path doesn’t match filter (predicate pushdown). scenario’s partition_by[[step]] picks task-table columns become partition levels step (§5). shard: One Parquet file disk — write-side concept. shard unit dispatch (one branch produces one shard), atomic rewrite (one Parquet rewritten task set changes, §8), caching (file existence partition path = cache hit). One shard contains 1+ task results, one row per task, task_id column. design one shard ≡ one partition leaf (part-N style splitting); leaf file always named part.parquet. sidecar: small auxiliary metadata file written beside data describes (directory), rather embedded — human-readable, diffable JSON kept separate bulk Parquet results. ssdsims writes two (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §8.5): per-scenario manifest (<results>/manifest.json — scenario’s declarative fields, session info, completed_shards), per-shard sha256 record (meta.json) written next shard’s part.parquet write time, recording shard’s trusted--produced sha256. One writer per sidecar file, parallel shard targets never race shared manifest; manifest assembler later unions per-shard sidecars completed_shards. shard sidecar uploaded Parquet, download can verified re-hashing recorded sha256. path axis / inner axis: two halves step’s task_axes(step) given partitioning. Path axes become Hive directory levels; inner axes complement — ordinary Parquet columns vary row--row inside shard. Together path ⊎ inner = task_axes(step). partition_by: per-step path axes — canonical sharding knob (scenario$partition_by, sample/fit/hc named list). partition_by[[step]] subset task_axes(step) whose values become shard’s Hive path, one shard per cell; shard count Π |path axis|. path axes → finer (, smaller) shards. stored source truth. bundle: per-step inner axes — complement partition_by (setdiff(task_axes(step), partition_by[[step]])): axes whose tasks kept together within one shard (bundled axes → coarser, larger files). equal-status alias entry point ssd_define_scenario(): per step caller names split (partition_by) keep together (bundle) — one two per step, two may mixed across steps (e.g. partition_by sample, bundle fit). ssdsims normalizes single complete, stored partition_by (defaults fill step named neither) recomputes bundle print(), shows . task identity vs shard path: task’s identity — <step>_id primary key <parent>_id foreign key — path_key() task_axes(step): unique per task, partition_by-independent, exactly per-task primer hashes foreign-key join uses. shard path coarser path_key() partition_by[[step]] , shared every task shard. coincide one-task-per-shard; changing partition_by (bundle) moves shard path, never identity, primer, results. step: One three RNG-touching stages pipeline: data (slice_sample_state()), fit (fit_dists_state()), hc (hc_state). step task table (data_tasks / fit_tasks / hc_tasks), grid, partition_by axes, dynamic-branched target (data_step / fit_step / hc_step), shard directory. word “step” reserved three stages. target: targets::tar_target() declaration _targets.R script. static target produces one object; dynamic-branched target pattern = map(grouped_tbl) produces one branch per group upstream grouped table. branch: One sub-target dynamic-branched target — produced pattern = map(...) iterated grouped task table. 1 branch = 1 group = 1 shard ; branch body loops K tasks group, primes RNG per task, writes one Parquet. Independent branches run parallel — unit parallelism design commits . branches packed Slurm jobs crew_controller_slurm() open question (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §11). job: Reserved exclusively cluster-scheduler term — Slurm (equivalent) work unit dispatched crew controller. job may host one branch several; precise branch ↔︎ job mapping crew_controller_slurm() open question (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §11). fixed independent shards run parallel — shard granularity unit parallelism, regardless branches packed jobs. title: ssdsims Glossary
text: three nest, finest coarsest: scenario one regular grid, design unions scenarios one pipeline run, study aggregates design-runs across infrastructure, time, software versions. scenario: single, purely declarative ssdsims_scenario (ssd_define_scenario()) — one regular cross-join axes step (rectangular sub-grid). construction-time root one targets pipeline (ssd_scenario_targets()), carrying seed, knobs, dataset names; draws random numbers expands tasks (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §1). Vignette prose calls scenario “study” loose gloss section tightens: scenario one arm study, study. design: named set scenarios run one pipeline — one targets store, one tar_make(), one provenance/execution context — built ssd_design() turned targets ssd_design_targets(). single scenario one regular grid, design unions several full, possibly non-regular (ragged) experimental design: union regular sub-grids exactly irregular design region expressed. “Design” used design--experiments sense (set conditions run); distinct software-design sense TARGETS-DESIGN.md openspec design.md artifact. scenario member design, addressed collection name (scenario=<name> results level <name>_ target-name prefix); names enter addressing , never task identity, primer, result value. design’s results table combined summary.parquet, scenario identity column. study: whole investigation design serves — longitudinal aggregate may span multiple design-runs executed different infrastructure (laptop / cluster), different times, different software versions. Unlike scenario design, study constructible object: members realised separate sessions, reconstructed read side unioning result trees (future study column several designs’ summaries). Reserved umbrella level; built design machinery. Distinct experiment, repo names proof--work scripts/experiment-*.R.

title: ssdsims Glossary
text: sim: index simulation replicate. nsim: number simulation replicates perform. nrow: number rows (species) simulated dataset. replace: Whether resampling generates simulated data performed replacement.

title: ssdsims Glossary
text: SSD: Species sensitivity distribution: distribution species-level toxicity endpoints used ecological risk assessment. dists: parametric distributions fit SSD data (e.g. lnorm, gamma, llogis); see ssdtools::ssd_fit_dists(). single character vector defining one model-averaged fit, applied uniformly every fit task — fit-level simulation setting (), cross-join axis: absent task_axes("fit"), never fans , enters primer, becomes partition level. (Fanning per-distribution dissolve model averaging defines fit.) fits: fitdists object holding one fitted distributions. hc: Hazard concentration: quantile SSD given proportion (e.g. hc5 5% quantile); see ssdtools::ssd_hc(). proportion: proportion species affected hazard concentration computed. ci: Scenario-wide scalar flag whether compute confidence intervals hazard concentrations. simulation setting, cross-join axis — point estimate identical whether ci TRUE FALSE, ci = TRUE superset ci = FALSE (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §1.2). ci_method: method used compute confidence intervals (e.g. multi_fixed, weighted_samples). nboot: number bootstrap replicates used computing confidence intervals. hc cross-join axis (task_axes("hc")): distinct values fan separate tasks, primers, shards, results can partitioned nboot. enters per-task primer, giving value independent, reproducible bootstrap draw (bootstrap RNG consumer hc estimation; point estimate analytic). See TARGETS-DESIGN.md §9 stays primer rather sharing one stream across nboot values.

what: 3. The sim column identity claim — confirmed with a caveat
title: RNG flow in ssdsims — from scripts/example.R
text: Claim (user’s question): sim column every row nested tibble identifies unique L’Ecuyer-CMRG substream, fixed (seed, stream, sim) state used ssd_sim_data(), fit_dists_seed() hc_seed(). Verdict — design intent: confirmed. three call sites pass (seed, stream, sim) triple get_lecuyer_cmrg_stream_state{,s}(), helper pure function three inputs. Empirical check (Rscript current main, seed = 42): substream lattice exactly canonical L’Ecuyer-CMRG one: Caveat — implementation bug uncovered verifying. check states_data[[1]] == fit_seed(sim=1) initially came back FALSE fresh R session. Root cause: get_lecuyer_cmrg_state() (R/lecuyer-cmrg-seed.R:156) calls RNGkind("L'Ecuyer-CMRG", …), mutates global RNG kind. get_lecuyer_cmrg_stream_states() wraps .exit(set_state(ostate)), .Random.seed exist call (get_state() returns NULL), set_state(NULL) documented -op — RNG kind restored. next call therefore enters kind already L’Ecuyer-CMRG, set.seed(seed) produces different starting state Mersenne-Twister. inputs, different output. normal usage practical impact small — anything session touched RNG, .Random.seed exists .exit restores properly — real reproducible clean R --vanilla. PoC fixes pinning RNGkind("Mersenne-Twister", …) set.seed(seed) inside get_lecuyer_cmrg_stream_states() (R/lecuyer-cmrg-seed.R:178 claude/adoring-ramanujan-rXimG), explanatory comment.

what: 4. Streams — concept vs. usage in ssdsims
title: RNG flow in ssdsims — from scripts/example.R
text: L’Ecuyer-CMRG defines two levels advancement: parallel::nextRNGStream(s) — jumps ~2^127 draws ahead; canonical way give different parallel workers statistically independent sequences. parallel::nextRNGSubStream(s) — jumps smaller (~2^76) amount; intended separate replicates within worker. ssdsims maps : stream concept used parallelism inside ssdsims? . parallel/furrr/mirai call package. Every call ssd_sim_data() / ssd_fit_dists_sims() / ssd_hc_sims() uses single scalar stream (default 1L, overridable via ssdsims.stream option explicit argument). package iterates purrr::pmap(), sequentially. stream actually , ? user-facing extensibility hook. calling user gets two batches whose substreams overlap (separated nextRNGStream, .e. ~2^127 draws), batches reproducible master seed. words, stream existing “want add sims without disturbing ones already ” axis — exactly lever user’s goal #3 (extensibility) wants push . just hasn’t wired workflow yet.

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: End--end workflow running ssdsims scenario cluster. Three primary goals, hard constraint: Reproducibility. scenario’s results bit-stable across reruns machines (RNG state explicit serializable; §1, §2). Debuggability. single failed task cluster must replayable locally — targets, orchestrator — using inputs cluster used. _state primitives (slice_sample_state(), fit_dists_state(), hc_state(), taking per-task primer) per-shard Parquet partitions manifest (§7). Extensibility. Shards cache unit: larger scenario reuses shards Parquets already computed computes rest. Path-axis growth adds new shards; inner-axis growth rewrites affected shards atomically (§8). Pinning outputs despite code change stays inside one project via tar_cue(depend = FALSE) — second project needed (§8.3). Parallelism assumed throughout. document covers, order: scenario object name-keyed datasets/min_pmix (§1), per-task dqrng+hash RNG mechanism (§2), running locally (§3), assembling cluster pipeline (§4), three step grids fan-outs (§5), concrete target graph cloud upload hook (§6), debugging cluster failure (§7), extension (§8). §9 lists known limitations, §10 maps gaps RNG-FLOW.md §5 resolutions, §11 collects open questions, §12 implementation roadmap. Terminology (per GLOSSARY.md): task one row {data,fit,hc}_tasks table — smallest unit computation, carrying primer; shard Parquet file produced running one several tasks (depending partitioning). Step = one three stages data / fit / hc; target = tar_target() declaration; job = Slurm work unit. shard named *_step target (minted tar_map construction time — static branching, §6); shard’s tasks run inside one target, writes 1 shard. what: Tasks bundle into shards by partition
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: Tasks unit computation (one row, one primer, one _state call). Shards unit storage, dispatch parallelism: one shard ≡ one Parquet file ≡ one named step target (minted tar_map, §6), independent shards run parallel. (shard targets packed Slurm jobs crew_controller_slurm() open question — see §11.) One shard typically contains many tasks: scenario’s partition_by[[step]] picks task-table columns become Hive directory levels, every task row sharing column values goes shard. Default partition_by: partition_by scenario-configurable; pushing axes path produces smaller, finer-grained shards; pushing fewer axes produces coarser shards. changes: Shard count Π |path axis| per step, Π |task axis|. §6 example (1 dataset · 2 sim · 2 nrow · 2 rescale · 2 est_method, default partition_by): 2 data shards, 4 fit shards, 2 hc shards (instead 4 / 8 / 16 tasks--shards). Per-task RNG unchanged. task shard runs primer; branch body loops shard’s task rows primes RNG per task. Cache granularity per shard, per task. Adding new inner-axis value (e.g. new min_pmix) changes task-row set every affected shard forces atomic rewrite Parquets (existing branches re-run). Adding new path-axis value (e.g. new dataset) creates new shards leaves existing shards untouched — see §8. Replay (§7) still per task. task’s primer, params, row’s upstream partition-path columns fully reproduce computation; shard happens live incidental. small nsim = 2, nrow = c(5, 10), rescale = c(F, T), est_method = c("arithmetic", "multi") example, default partition_by (§5): (legacy scripts/example-expanded-grids-independent.R allocated 28 treating nrow independent axis — design longer ; see scripts/experiment-subset-property.R proof nrow sub-truncation.)

what: Static branching: one named target per shard
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: shard set pure function scenario, scenario known _targets.R sourced — plain constructor call top file, tar_target(). fan-width fixed construction time, never data-dependent run time. standard rule thumb — static branching branch count known sourcing time, dynamic branching depends data computed run — design uses static branching: tarchetypes::tar_map() mints one named target per shard pipeline built. project’s targets lab (set single-behaviour toy pipelines sister planning repo) exercised styles head head — static-branch axis dynamic-branch axis pair compare: fan-shape, decided construction time vs run time — confirmed rule applies . complete pipeline (pinning + error = "null" + per-shard upload, composed) built static branching. static fits design specifically: scenario need live target. construction-time object; ssd_scenario_*_shards(scenario) evaluate sourcing time feed tar_map()’s values. targets still tracks scenario referenced global, editing knob still invalidates dependent shards — tracking lost taking target. Named, addressable shards. shard DAG node (fit_step_boron_sim1_rescaleFALSE), tar_read() / tar_invalidate() (§8.3–§8.4) replay story (§7) can point one shard name, tar_mermaid() shows real per-shard graph per-shard status colours. Extension literally “named targets.” Adding dataset growing nsim (path-axis growth, §8.1) mints new shard targets next sourcing; targets diffs target set builds new ones — exactly §8 cache story, directory-hash indirection needed keep existing shards rebuilding. Precise upstream edges. fit shard target can name one upstream data shard target directly, changed data shard invalidates dependent fit/hc shards — strictly better depending whole pattern target. (Hive partition-path layout still kept, -cluster query layer replay, inter-target wiring crutch dynamic branching force.) bounded exception — scale. tar_map() builds one target object per shard every time _targets.R sourced, scenario many shards (thousands: many datasets × large nsim fine-grained partition_by) makes sourcing graph tooling heavy, dynamic branching’s single pattern node stays light. Branching implementation detail behind shard abstraction — one Parquet per shard, identical per-task RNG, partition-path linking, replay contract either way — step whose fan-large enough hurt may opt dynamic branching (tar_target(pattern = map(<step>_tasks)) grouped task-table target) without changing anything else. Static default; dynamic documented escape hatch extreme fan-outs. targets lab validated. lab isolates targets behaviour laptop-runnable toy pipeline; three findings branching axes carry back : static default holds, cheap-extension payoff real. grow--pipeline spike bumps variant count 4 -> 6 re-runs: existing four variants skipped (commands unchanged never depended count), extending costs build new variants + redo fan-, redo everything. precisely path-axis-growth story design leans (§8.1) — confirmed, assumed. Static mints separately-named, addressable nodes. tar_map() produces N separately-named targets, tar_read()-addressable DAG node, matching named-shard rationale ; dynamic branching instead tracks branches hashed names downstream target row-binds automatically. escape hatch works; just trades addressability single light pattern node. shard just multi-row target value. sharding spike confirms shard target simply target returns many rows; branching axes add ways generate targets — branching stays implementation detail behind shard abstraction, claimed. link steps upstream partition path: shard row carries partition column values upstream shard needs, body opens one upstream Parquet. static branching shard target can additionally name specific upstream shard target precise dependency edge; partition path remains portable contract used -cluster query layer (Hive-style layout ) replay (§7). lets tweaking rescale re-run fit + hc without re-running data (fit shard’s data partition columns unchanged). _targets.R sketch: summary reads three result directories directly duckplyr rather depending shard target — sees whatever shards landed (survivors included, §6.2) pull every shard’s value back R. tarchetypes::tar_combine() alternative fan--memory bind wanted instead. step body (sketch): body loops per task shard, primes RNG task’s primer, calls (state-less) _state primitive, stacks K result rows one Parquet shard’s partition path. fit shard opens upstream data shard(s) tasks reference — single shard, general set parent shards child spans (§5/§6 Linking), read filtered via duckplyr — fit shard never depends whole data step. static branching scoping computed sourcing time: fit shard target names upstream data shard target(s) (Option 3, §12 task-tables) precise per-shard edges, adding new data shards mints new targets leaves existing fit shards untouched — directory-hash indirection required avoid spurious rebuilds. Dependencies re-runs knob change (applied 2/4/2 shard counts ; “1 shard re-runs” = Parquet rewritten new task set): Three steps three targets makes matrix possible: single combined branch (data + fit + hc) cache fit shard nboot changes. Layout coherence across re-runs. shard’s Hive path depth axes function partition_by/bundle, readers glob <step>/**/part.parquet (depth-agnostic). changing split re-running one fixed results/ leave shards different granularity beside new ones, glob union stale current shards — double-counting, since <step>_id identity partition_by-independent (one task lands old coarse new fine shard). Two complementary fixes, driver: Single-core (ssd_run_scenario_shards) — tree. clears <dir>/<step> writing, tree always matches current layout. cache preserve, clobbering simplest correct fix. targets — per-layout root. layout writes scenario_results_dir(scenario) = results/layout=<hash(partition_by)>, split change yields fresh root (never mixed old) layout reuses root (cache + error = "null" survivors preserved). Non-layout knobs (seed, grids) keep root just rewrite shard paths depth (§8.1). Pruning orphaned layout root manifest-driven reads deferred manifest/shard-atomic-rewrite. Available analysis: tar_make(), three step layers queryable independently via duckplyr without going targets (Hive-partitioned reads auto-discover partition columns path): Path-axis filters push directory tree (irrelevant Parquets opened); inner-axis filters (e.g. min_pmix == "default") push row-group metadata still need open relevant Parquets.
code: scenario   (plain construction-time object; carries seed, partition_by)        │        ├──▶ data_shards  ( 2 rows ; partition cols dataset,sim,replace)        │         │        │         ▼  tar_map(values = data_shards, names = c(dataset, sim, replace))        │     data_step_*  ──▶ results/data/dataset=boron/sim=1/replace=FALSE/part.parquet  (2 named shard targets)        │        ├──▶ fit_shards   ( 4 rows ; partition cols dataset,sim,rescale; data upstream)        │         │        │         ▼  tar_map(values = fit_shards, names = c(dataset, sim, rescale))        │     fit_step_*   ──▶ results/fit/dataset=boron/sim=1/rescale=FALSE/part.parquet   (4 named shard targets)        │                  each shard reads the matching data shard by partition path        │        └──▶ hc_shards    ( 2 rows ; partition cols dataset,sim; fit upstream)                  │                  ▼  tar_map(values = hc_shards, names = c(dataset, sim))              hc_step_*  ──▶ results/hc/dataset=boron/sim=1/part.parquet                  (2 named shard targets, 4 tasks each)                          each shard reads the matching fit shard(s) by partition path     summary  ──▶ results/summary.parquet                 (reads all three layers via duckplyr) library(targets) library(tarchetypes)  # The scenario is a plain object built HERE, at sourcing time — not a # tar_target. Everything downstream is a pure function of it, so the # shard set is fixed before any target runs (static branching). scenario <- ssd_scenario(   ssddata::ccme_boron,   nsim = 2L,   nrow = c(5L, 10L),   rescale = c(FALSE, TRUE),   est_method = c("arithmetic", "multi"),   nboot = 10,   seed = 42L )  # One row per shard, computed now (sourcing time). Each row carries the # step's partition_by values (which become the tar_map target-name # suffix and the Hive path) plus a list-column `tasks` of the task rows # that shard must run. data_shards <- ssd_scenario_data_shards(scenario)  # 2 rows fit_shards  <- ssd_scenario_fit_shards(scenario)   # 4 rows hc_shards   <- ssd_scenario_hc_shards(scenario)    # 2 rows (4 tasks each)  list(   # tar_map mints one named target per shard at sourcing time. Independent   # shard targets run in parallel; how they pack into Slurm jobs under   # crew_controller_slurm() is an open question (§11).   #   # error = "null" so one bad shard does not abort the run: the target   # goes NULL, the error is recorded, the rest keeps building and summary   # unions the survivors (§6.2). format = "file" passes the shard path,   # not its value, between targets (see below).   tar_map(     values = data_shards, names = c(dataset, sim, replace),     tar_target(       data_step,       ssd_run_data_step(tasks, scenario, out_dir = "results/data"),       format = "file", error = "null"     )   ),    tar_map(     values = fit_shards, names = c(dataset, sim, rescale),     tar_target(       fit_step,       ssd_run_fit_step(tasks, scenario,                        data_dir = "results/data",                        out_dir  = "results/fit"),       format = "file", error = "null"     )   ),    tar_map(     values = hc_shards, names = c(dataset, sim),     tar_target(       hc_step,       ssd_run_hc_step(tasks, scenario,                       fit_dir = "results/fit",                       out_dir = "results/hc"),       format = "file", error = "null"     )   ),    tar_target(     summary,     ssd_summarise(dir_data = "results/data",                   dir_fit  = "results/fit",                   dir_hc   = "results/hc",                   path     = "results/summary.parquet"),     format = "file"   ) ) # `tasks` is the shard row's list-column of task rows (one per task in # this shard), supplied by tar_map from fit_shards. ssd_run_fit_step <- function(tasks, scenario, data_dir, out_dir) {   rows <- purrr::pmap_dfr(tasks, function(task_id, primer, data_id,                                           rescale, min_pmix, ...) {     upstream <- duckplyr::read_parquet_duckdb(file.path(data_dir,                                               # partition path from upstream cols                                               .upstream_path(...),                                               "part.parquet"))     local_dqrng_state(scenario$seed, primer)     tibble::tibble(task_id = task_id,                    rescale = rescale,                    min_pmix = min_pmix,                    fit = list(fit_dists_state(upstream, rescale, min_pmix, ...)))   })   out <- file.path(out_dir,                    .partition_path(tasks, scenario$partition_by$fit),                    "part.parquet")   dir.create(dirname(out), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)   duckplyr::compute_parquet(rows, out)   out } duckplyr::read_parquet_duckdb("results/data/**/*.parquet") |>   dplyr::filter(dataset == "boron", sim == 1L) |> dplyr::collect() duckplyr::read_parquet_duckdb("results/fit/**/*.parquet")  |> ... duckplyr::read_parquet_duckdb("results/hc/**/*.parquet")   |> ...

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: data/fit/hc shards user-facing artefacts need readable outside cluster — analysis notebooks laptop, dashboards, downstream R/Python scripts. Implemented (cloud-upload capability) three departures original sketch : destination runner argument ssd_scenario_targets(scenario, ..., root, upload, cue) (sibling root), scenario field; typed, self-validating object rather untyped list; absent Azure credentials fail loud rather silently dry-running. See realised contract immediately ; historical sketch follows context. destination typed runner argument. Two constructors return plain, serialisable, classed S3 objects carrying destination (never credentials): ssd_upload_azure(url, container, ..., prefix = NULL, domain = "blob.core.windows.net") (class c("ssdsims_upload_azure_blob", "ssdsims_upload"); storage account derived url, optional prefix writes shards subdirectory container) ssd_upload_dryrun() (class c("ssdsims_upload_dryrun", "ssdsims_upload")), validated construction. passed name factory — upload = NULL (default; upload_<step> nodes), ssd_upload_dryrun() (-op nodes, exercised offline/CI), ssd_upload_azure(...) (ship Azure). Four generics dispatch object’s class: ssd_test_upload() (front-door creds/connectivity probe, user’s explicit preflight — factory never runs ), ssd_upload_shard(path, upload) (ship one shard), ssd_open_uploaded(upload, step) (read uploaded results back place), ssd_summarise_uploaded(upload, step, drop_samples) (-place fan-summary, cloud counterpart ssd_summarise()). new backend (S3/GCS) constructor plus four methods — edit existing methods. Fail loud absent credentials. Azure missing credentials errors (naming missing SSDSIMS_AZURE_* variable), probe time per-shard backstop — never silent -op. Intent skip network expressed ssd_upload_dryrun(). Upload target, inline side effect. upload non-NULL factory adds one upload_<step> target per shard, paired step target tar_map: targets lab’s Azure spike showed beats pushing blob inside ssd_run_<step>_step() right local write: Content-hashing skips unchanged shards. upload_<step> takes shard’s path (format = "file") input, targets re-runs shard’s content hash changes. re-driven tar_make() rebuilt nothing uploads nothing; partial extension uploads new/rewritten shards. (combined lab pipeline composes error = "null" pinning cue: fixing one failed branch becomes minimal re-upload, full redo.) graph builds dry-runs credentials — explicitly. Passing ssd_upload_dryrun() gives upload_<step> nodes -op (reach network return local path), DAG shape runs offline CI without credentials. now explicit opt-, silent fallback absent credentials (see fail loud ): ssd_upload_azure(...) missing credentials errors. Concerns stay separated dependency. compute manifest depends shard targets (produced); upload manifest depends upload_<step> targets (shipped). reader needs uploaded data depends latter. Read shards back place, download. Azure spike closes loop -cluster read story: query step reads uploaded blobs place Azure DuckDB’s azure extension (Hive-glob az://…/<partition>=*/part.parquet) — analysis layer never downloads Parquet, predicate-pushes straight blob storage. Hive partition layout () pays identically whether shards sit cluster filesystem object store. Per-shard flow upload non-NULL: local shard stays disk targets’ format = "file" tracking unaffected; cloud copy additional artefact. Auth external. storage account derived destination url (environment variable); secret comes environment (one SSDSIMS_AZURE_STORAGE_KEY, SSDSIMS_AZURE_STORAGE_SAS, service-principal trio). upload object carry secrets — carries destination (URL, container, optional prefix/domain, account derived url). missing secret loud error (naming missing variable), silent -op — skipping network ssd_upload_dryrun()’s job. Connectivity probe — user’s explicit preflight. ssd_test_upload(upload) performs minimal round-trip (list container, write delete small marker blob) either returns silently errors backend’s diagnostic — resolving credentials first aborting, naming missing SSDSIMS_AZURE_* variable, one absent. one-liner user runs prompt tar_make() answer “credentials right place?”. factory ssd_scenario_targets() run : _targets.R re-sourced every targets operation (tar_make(), also tar_manifest(), tar_visnetwork(), tar_outdated()) every worker crew/cluster run, probing factory fire credential resolution marker-blob round-trip — surprising network /O every graph inspection N× cluster, requirement workers carry credentials merely source pipeline. factory therefore stays pure, side-effect-free assembler; fail-loud preserved backstop ssd_upload_shard() (missing credential aborts shard’s upload branch upload time, leaving rest shipping error = "null"). Failure mode. per-shard upload error becomes upload_<step> branch’s error (, error = "null", leaves rest uploading); local shard remains, tar_make() can re-driven failed uploads retried. upload hashing now. upload path records sha256 — shard shipped read back place (ssd_open_uploaded() / ssd_summarise_uploaded()), row data round-trip check. Hash-based transfer-corruption detection (upload sha256, shipping manifest’s per-shard meta.json sidecar alongside blob) deferred together parked manifest concept, revisited manifest picked (manifest-revival task ROADMAP.md).

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: single bad shard must abort N-shard cluster run, single bad task must lose rest shard. design splits two concerns — tolerant producer separate completeness gate: shard body writes many rows ran successfully. shard runs K bundled tasks, keeps survivors, writes one Parquet; task fails simply absent. partial failure therefore yields shorter shard, error, step target fails exceptional circumstances (upstream shard unreadable, worker OOMs, package won’t load). carries light error = "null" catastrophic cases — ordinary partial-failure path longer depends . summary unions whatever rows land. downstream assertion target makes shortfall visible. Sibling upload_<step> (§6.1), shard gets assert_<step> target reads shard file’s row count compares expected count — pure function scenario’s task table, known _targets.R sourced read Parquet footer (scan). errors (goes red) mismatch, completeness first-class, coloured DAG node: green = full shard, red = short shard, queryable via tar_meta() visible tar_mermaid(). assert_<step> carries error = "null" one red assert abort build. expected count also catches silent shape bugs (join drops rows), just task failures. “see partial shard failures clearly” assert layer’s job; “keep going despite ” tolerant body’s. errored shard (exceptional case) always date, re-driven tar_make() fix retries (§8.4); short shard found red assert_<step> (also §8.4). Keep-going pipeline default (make -k), layered. shipped _targets.R templates set tar_option_set(error = "continue") — make -k analogue — target errors skips dependents every reachable shard still builds; one bad branch never tears long parallel run. (pipeline-wide option, sits _targets.R next controller block, ssd_scenario_targets() factory — owns per-target settings instead.) shard, assert_<step>, upload_<step> targets carry stronger per-target error = "null", permissive make -k: errored target’s value becomes NULL downstream still runs (short/NULL shard flows reported, ), plain make -k skip . two settings compose cleanly — per-target error overrides global default — global continue governs targets carry explicit override (e.g. unexpected error summary user-added target skips dependents instead aborting run). Guards abort whole run — upload/cluster pre-flight (§6.1, §4) — deliberately live outside DAG, separate pre-flight script user runs tar_make() (documented pre-condition, target pipeline enforces), keep-going default never swallows . trade accept: keep-going systemic failure (every shard red package won’t load) stop pipeline — operator watches first minutes, wall red surfaces immediately, optimising losing long run one bad branch beats stop--first-error. Override explicit tar_option_set(error = "stop") _targets.R hard stop ever wanted. Two constraints, confirmed targets lab’s failure spike: Errors read run, inside . target call tar_meta() (store functions) pipeline running. summary detects gap missing shard / NULL value inside DAG, error messages pulled metadata tar_make() returns: end--pipeline warning needs handling, silencing. Even error = "null", tar_make() emits “targets errored” warning end; options(warn = 2) becomes abort. runner wraps tar_make() withCallingHandlers() muffles one expected warning rather relaxing global option. short NULL shard must flow cleanly downstream. shorter shard still valid Parquet, upload_<step> ships manifests record normal; assert_<step> goes red. exceptional case whole shard errors value NULL, immediate-downstream targets — upload_<step>, assert_<step>, two manifests — must tolerate rather choke: ssd_upload_shard(NULL, ...) records skip, assert_<step> treats missing file zero rows (red), manifests omit . combined lab pipeline composed construction-time-sized fan-(static branching), error = "null" handling, per-shard upload target, found tolerance upload manifest helpers glue composition needed — failing branch fixed §8.3 pin, one shard rebuilds, re-uploads, re-queried (minimal re-run, full redo). split producer assertion. Folding row-count check shard body make short shard error, error = "null" discards hard-won survivor rows , §8.3 pin, also force rebuild next tar_make(). Keeping check separate downstream target lets survivors persist stay queryable red assert_<step> flags gap — completeness reported, enforced destruction. cost errored downstream assert invalidate upstream shard, refreshing short shard deliberate step, automatic; §8.4 shows runner drives red-assert set.

code: 1. Identify the failing task row.       ──────────────────────────────       # Find the task that errored within the failing branch:       # branch error message names the shard's partition path;       # within the shard the task_id of the bad row is in the log.       task <- tar_read(hc_tasks) |> dplyr::filter(task_id == "3ab9c7")        The row carries:         - primer    (length-2 integer = c(hi31, lo31), §2)         - all hc params (nboot, est_method, ci_method, ...)         - upstream fit-shard partition values (dataset, sim, rescale)           — enough to reconstruct results/fit/dataset=.../sim=.../rescale=.../part.parquet     2. Locate the upstream shard by partition path.       ────────────────────────────────────────────       results/fit/dataset=<...>/sim=<...>/rescale=<...>/part.parquet       The immediate upstream is enough; no need to walk further.     3. Sync to local.       ──────────────       rsync cluster:_targets/objects/hc_tasks   ./_targets/objects/       rsync cluster:<upstream_path>             ./<upstream_path>     4. Reproduce, no targets involved.       ───────────────────────────────       fit <- duckplyr::read_parquet_duckdb(<upstream_path>) |>              # the fit shard may contain multiple fit task rows;              # pick the one this hc task points to.              dplyr::filter(task_id == task$fit_id) |>              dplyr::pull(fit) |> .subset2(1L)       options(error = recover)        # or place browser() in hc_state       out <- ssdsims:::hc_state(         data       = fit,         seed       = scenario$seed,         state      = task$primer[[1]],         nboot      = task$nboot,         est_method = task$est_method,         ci_method  = task$ci_method,         proportion = scenario$hc$proportion,         ci         = scenario$hc$ci,         parametric = task$parametric,         save_to    = NULL       )

what: 8.4 Forced re-run after a code fix
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: dual case: bug hc_state() fixed; user wants buggy shards rewritten patched code. Partition paths unchanged (fix doesn’t alter partition_by columns), file existence still says “cache hit”. what: 8.6 The incremental loop — run, assemble, expand, re-assemble
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: pieces compose one repeatable cycle: run scenario, assemble, expand scenario, run missing pieces, assemble — redundant recomputation. works every step pure function current state, never accumulation edits: Run. tar_make() writes one Parquet per shard scenario’s layout root (§5). Path-axis growth inner-axis rewrite (§8.1–§8.2) exactly rules decide shards later run rebuilds. Assemble. ssd_assemble_manifest() walks results tree rebuilds completed_shards whatever Parquets exist (§8.5), preferring shard’s meta.json sidecar hashing rest. reads manifest head left ssd_write_manifest() replaces tail. Expand. Edit scenario object — append dataset, grow nsim, add min_pmix, widen nrow — re-source _targets.R. shard set re-derived expanded scenario sourcing time (§6 static branching). Run missing pieces. tar_make() . Path-axis growth mints builds new shards skips rest (cache-- existence, §8.1); inner-axis growth atomically rewrites affected shards (§8.2). Nothing else recomputes. Assemble . ssd_assemble_manifest() re-walks tree. rebuilds tail disk every call, second assembly idempotent monotone growth: new shards appear, untouched ones keep (byte-identical) entries, result union — merge bookkeeping, stale entries prune. Two ordering facts make loop safe. Write head assemble. head pure function scenario (§8.5), ssd_write_manifest() rewrites whole file head alone — preserve previous tail. expansion head must re-written expanded scenario (otherwise still describes smaller grid), assemble must follow rebuild tail disk. runner’s contract therefore write-head--assemble-tail every (re-)run: head always matches current scenario, tail always matches shards disk, two drift across runs (within-run case §6 static branching). Growth keeps layout root; re-layout starts fresh tree. results root keyed partition_by (layout=<hash>, §5), growing along existing axes accretes tree reuses cache manifest. Changing partition_by growth — mints new layout root (initially empty) manifest, leaving old tree intact rather migrating .

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: dists controls distributions ssdtools::ssd_fit_dists() fits given data slice; nboot controls many bootstrap iterations ssdtools::ssd_hc() runs. loops live inside ssdtools exposed ssdsims level, neither nesting — c("lnorm", "gamma") ⊂ c("lnorm", "gamma", "llogis"), nboot = 100 ⊂ nboot = 1000 — can reused incrementally: larger run re-fits re-draws scratch rather extending cached smaller one. Exploiting nesting require either () wrapping per-distribution / per-bootstrap iteration ssdsims dqrng stream aggregating, (b) ssdtools change expose inner loops. Sketch ; scope. two knobs sit opposite sides axis/setting split (GLOSSARY.md), cache behaviour differs accordingly — heading “neither axis”: dists fit-level simulation setting, axis. single character vector applied uniformly every fit task (one model-averaged ssd_fit_dists() call per task); absent task_axes("fit"), never enters primer partition. dists vector defines one model-averaged fit, fanning per-distribution change science, deliberately axis. Editing (e.g. adding llogis) changes content fed every fit task re-fits whole slice — “rewrite fit shards” row §8 — way fit added distribution merge. nboot hc grid axis (task_axes("hc")): distinct values fan separate tasks, identities, shards, cached independently partition_by[["hc"]] may list nboot shard . Raising nboot invalidate existing branch — smaller value keeps identity shard (nsim-grow story, §8.1); unavailable content reuse across nboot values. nboot enters per-task primer (nrow ). Independent-stream-per-nboot honest, robust default; shared-stream nesting revisitable ssdtools exposes loop prefix-stability validated pinned.

title: Uploading Shards to Cloud Storage
text: ssd_upload_azure() ssd_upload_dryrun() return plain, classed S3 objects carry destination — never credentials, connections, environments — travel unchanged crew workers targets: Credentials stay external: Azure methods resolve secret environment call time — one SSDSIMS_AZURE_STORAGE_KEY, SSDSIMS_AZURE_STORAGE_SAS, service-principal trio (SSDSIMS_AZURE_TENANT_ID/SSDSIMS_AZURE_CLIENT_ID/SSDSIMS_AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET) — object holds secrets. storage account name derived url (acct https://acct.blob.core.windows.net), account environment variable; sovereign cloud, set domain (e.g. domain = "blob.core.usgovcloudapi.net"). write subdirectory container (one container can hold several independent result sets), pass prefix: shards land <container>/<prefix>/<step>/<partition>/part.parquet, ssd_open_uploaded() reads back prefixed location.

title: Estimating a Scenario’s Compute Cost
text: scenario declarative: knobs can fan multi-day run warning. motivating example ssd_define_scenario()’s docs — 10 simulations × 4 sample sizes × 7 ci_methods × 4 nboot values (50 000), ci = TRUE — measured roughly 430 single-core hours (18 days), dominated hazard-concentration (hc) bootstrap, single multi_free + nboot = 50 000 task taking 44 minutes . launching run want two numbers: total compute costs (size budget), single longest task (irreducible wall-time floor — amount parallelism finishes job faster slowest task). ssd_estimate_cost() predicts reading scenario’s task expansion applying calibrated cost model. never fits distribution, runs bootstrap, draws random number, writes file.

title: Estimating a Scenario’s Compute Cost
text: Session benchmarking established ci = TRUE hc bootstrap dominates: fit data-sampling steps comparatively cheap. Per-task time follows simple model, fit separately ci_method: time=(base+slope×max⁡(nboot,n0))×nrow_factor(nrow) \text{time} = \bigl(\text{base} + \text{slope} \times \max(\text{nboot}, n_0)\bigr) \times \text{nrow\_factor}(\text{nrow}) Three properties make tractable: proportion est_method free. One bootstrap per nboot × ci_method × parametric cell serves every proportion est_method, adding values along axes multiply cost. (proportion vectorised single ssd_hc() call; est_method post-hoc aggregation bootstrap.) bootstrap floor n0. n0 draws per-call time roughly constant (fixed overhead dominates); time grows linearly nboot. model uses max(nboot, n0). bounded, non-monotonic nrow factor. Sample size weak, data-dependent effect — cheap nrow = 5 (bcanz distributions fail fit), peaking around 10–20, easing 50. captured bounded lookup, extrapolated linear term, least precise part model. per-ci_method slopes span roughly 9× — weighted_samples cheapest, multi_* methods expensive — ci_method grid far dominant cost driver. one-time research discovered model’s form (axes free, max(nboot, n0) shape, non-monotonic nrow factor) preserved change’s exploration/ directory. scripts illustrative; never rerun . Recalibrating new machine single function call, described .

title: Estimating a Scenario’s Compute Cost
text: coefficients architecture-specific — slope measured one CPU match another. package therefore ships default calibration (fitted development) method re-measure . default returned ssd_cost_calibration(): printed object carries fitted per-ci_method coefficients, nrow factor, fixed sample+fit per-task addend, provenance — CPU, R version, ssdtools version, date, sweep grid measured . stale default therefore visible estimate built , printed caveat explicit: estimate ballpark.
code: calibration <- ssd_cost_calibration() calibration #> <ssdsims_cost_calibration> #>   per-ci_method cost model  time = (base + slope * max(nboot, n0)) * nrow_factor #>     weighted_samples   base   0.58s     5.50 ms/boot  n0   0 #>     arithmetic_samples base   1.00s    18.01 ms/boot  n0  25 #>     MACL               base   0.92s    18.35 ms/boot  n0  25 #>     GMACL              base   0.91s    18.36 ms/boot  n0  25 #>     geometric_samples  base   0.95s    18.48 ms/boot  n0  25 #>     multi_free         base   0.18s    40.05 ms/boot  n0   0 #>     multi_fixed        base   0.15s    40.68 ms/boot  n0   0 #>   nrow_factor:   5:0.27  10:1.09  20:1.34  50:0.82 #>   fixed_addend:  0.05s (sample + fit per task) #>   provenance: #>     cpu:       Intel(R) Xeon(R) Processor @ 2.10GHz #>     R:         R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11) #>     ssdtools:  2.6.0.9002 #>     date:      2026-06-07 #>     sweep:     nboot {20, 50, 100, 200} x nrow {5, 10, 20, 50} x 7 ci_methods #>   Ballpark only: coefficients are architecture-specific - recalibrate with #>   ssd_calibrate_cost() on the target machine for a trustworthy estimate.

title: Estimating a Scenario’s Compute Cost
text: ssd_estimate_cost() takes scenario (optionally) calibration. motivating ~430-hour scenario: estimate reports serial total longest single task, time quantities, plus breakdown ci_method × nboot ordered total cost — can see glance cells dominate (, multi_* methods nboot = 50000). exact figures track calibration’s machine; different CPU, recalibrate () trustworthy numbers. proportion est_method free, widening leaves total unchanged: estimator deliberately reports serial numbers. n parallel workers, wall-time roughly longest task floor: split, sets minimum wall-time per-shard time budget regardless many workers throw job.

title: Defining a Scenario
text: ssdsims moving running simulation study immediately (legacy ssd_run_scenario()) cluster-friendly targets pipeline. root pipeline scenario: small, purely declarative description study want run. contains knobs — seed, number simulations, sample sizes, dataset names, fit/hc argument grids. draws random numbers, expands tasks, writes nothing, dependency targets. Keeping scenario declarative buys two things: serialises compact manifest — data frames, RNG state, function bodies — can shipped cluster stored alongside results. set work pipeline expands pure function scenario, scenario always describes study. vignette walks stages work today, order. intended grow: roadmap features land (per-task seeding, shards, targets backend), new sections document . Run baseline -process loop ssd_run_scenario_baseline(). Stages 1–3 side-effect-free. stage 4 touches RNG.

title: Defining a Scenario
text: ssd_data() single entry point dataset input. validates data frame — every dataset must carry numeric Conc column, species sensitivity distribution convention — assembles named collection. what: partition_by and bundle: how tasks group into shards
title: Running a Sharded Pipeline

title: Running a Sharded Pipeline
text: sharded runner single core. run shards parallel (cluster), building blocks plug static-branching targets pipeline: tarchetypes::tar_map() mints one named target per shard, targets caches, invalidates, reruns shard independently. Three ready example projects ship package — minimal small one, fuller large one (adapted scripts/example.R, sweeping nrow, proportion, estimation / CI methods), cluster one SLURM. large project parallelises shards across local workers mirai-backed crew controller (tar_option_set(controller = crew::crew_controller_local())); cluster project pipeline crew.cluster::crew_controller_slurm() controller, connectivity + worker-prerequisite preflight — see “Running SLURM Cluster” vignette, “zero running cluster job” guide maps site’s SLURM instructions onto controller: small large directories hold scenario.R (study, shared drivers), _targets.R (pipeline), run.R (targets driver), run-serial.R (single-core driver); cluster directory minimal four-file variant (scenario inline _targets.R, separate controller.R holds SLURM controller). whole _targets.R just build scenario call ssd_scenario_targets() factory — — editing study means editing scenario.R . Copy directory’s files project root, edit scenario.R, run either driver (targets path needs targets/tarchetypes Suggests): ssd_scenario_targets() mints one format = "file" target per shard (targets passes shard path, value, steps) error = "null" (failing whole shard records error goes NULL without aborting run — rest still build summary unions survivors), wires step ordering tar_combine() barriers (directory read carries automatic dependency edge). shard’s Hive-path depth depends partition_by/bundle, replaying changed split must leave stale-granularity shards beside new ones (** glob union ). two drivers handle differently: run.R (targets) writes layout root, scenario_results_dir(scenario) = results/layout=<hash>/, split change fresh tree; run-serial.R (single core) instead owns dir clears step subtree every run. drivers source("scenario.R") — study — partition_by free re-layout, run-serial.R finishes reading back summary.parquet targets scenario_results_dir(scenario) summary asserting per-task estimates byte-identical. Whatever driver — baseline, single-core shards, targets — results ; choose one fits run.
code: list.files(system.file("targets-templates", "small", package = "ssdsims")) #> [1] "_targets.R"   "run-serial.R" "run.R"        "scenario.R" library(targets) library(tarchetypes) library(ssdsims) source("scenario.R") # builds `scenario` ssd_scenario_targets(scenario) # the entire target list # install.packages(c("targets", "tarchetypes")) dir <- system.file("targets-templates", "small", package = "ssdsims") file.copy(list.files(dir, full.names = TRUE), ".")  source("run.R") # targets: tar_make() -> one target per shard -> results/<step>/... # or, from a shell:  Rscript run.R  source("run-serial.R") # single core via ssd_run_scenario_shards() -> results-serial/ # ...and, if results/ exists, it asserts the two drivers' estimates are identical

what: Calibrate the Per-task Cost Model on the Current Machine — ssd_calibrate_cost
title: Calibrate the Per-task Cost Model on the Current Machine — ssd_calibrate_cost
text: Runs small, fixed benchmark sweep current architecture - tiny nboot values, ssdtools::ssd_ci_methods(), couple nrow values - times ssdtools::ssd_hc() call, fits per-ci_method cost model time = (base + slope * max(nboot, n0)) * nrow_factor(nrow), returns versioned ssdsims_cost_calibration object carrying fitted coefficients provenance. Producing architecture-specific estimator nothing single call plus passing result ssd_estimate_cost().

text: x object use scenario. ... Unused. nrow positive whole number minimum number non-missing rows. replace logical vector specifying whether sample replacement. dists character vector distribution names. rescale flag specifying whether leave values unchanged (FALSE) rescale concentration values dividing geometric mean minimum maximum positive finite values (TRUE) string specifying whether leave values unchanged ("") rescale concentration values dividing geometric mean minimum maximum positive finite values ("geomean") logistically transform ("odds"). computable flag specifying whether return fits numerically computable standard errors. at_boundary_ok flag specifying whether model one parameters boundary considered converged (default = TRUE). min_pmix number 0 0.5 specifying minimum proportion mixture models. range_shape1 numeric vector length two lower upper bounds shape1 parameter burrIII3 distribution. range_shape2 numeric vector length two lower upper bounds shape2 parameter burrIII3 distribution. proportion numeric vector proportion values estimate hazard concentrations . ci flag specifying whether estimate confidence intervals (bootstrapping). nboot count number bootstrap samples use estimate confidence limits. value 10,000 recommended official guidelines. est_method string specifying whether estimate directly model-averaged cumulative distribution function (est_method = 'multi') take arithmetic mean estimates individual cumulative distribution functions weighted AICc derived weights  (est_method = 'arithmetic') use geometric mean instead (est_method = 'geometric'). ci_method string specifying method use estimating standard error confidence limits bootstrap samples. default recommended value still ci_method = "weighted_samples" takes bootstrap samples distribution proportional AICc based weights calculates confidence limits (SE) single set. ci_method = "multi_fixed"  ci_method = "multi_free" generate bootstrap samples using model-averaged cumulative distribution function differ whether model weights fixed values original dataset re-estimated bootstrap sample dataset. value ci_method = "MACL" (ci_method = "weighted_arithmetic"), included historical reasons, takes weighted arithmetic mean confidence limits ci_method = GMACL takes weighted geometric mean confidence limits added completeness also recommended. Finally ci_method = "arithmetic_samples" ci_method = "geometric_samples" take weighted arithmetic geometric mean values bootstrap iteration across distributions calculate confidence limits (SE) single set samples. parametric flag specifying whether perform parametric bootstrapping opposed non-parametrically resampling original data replacement. seed integer starting seed NULL. nsim count number data sets generate. stream count stream number. start_sim count number simulation start . .progress Whether show purrr::progress bar. dist_sim character vector specifying distributions fitdists object ""`` distributions /"top"use distribution weight /"multi"` treat distributions single distribution. args named list argument values.

title: Derive a Per-task Primer from its Parameters — task_primer

title: Derive a Per-task Primer from its Parameters — task_primer
text: primer packs 64 bits rlang::hash() digest (xxhash128) c(hi32, lo32). 32-bit half encoded signed int32, reserved bit pattern 0x80000000 (INT_MIN, R represent non-NA integer) mapped NA_integer_; dqrng accepts NA_integer_ stream treats INT_MIN, encoding recovers full 64 bits stream entropy. params may plain named list single-row data frame (one row {sample,fit,hc}_tasks table). data-frame row normalised canonical plain list – inverse tibble::tibble_row() – dropping attributes, unwrapping length-1 list-style columns element, leaving df-style (nested data-frame) columns data frames, hashing. primer therefore identical whether derived row equivalent plain list. Note rlang::hash() order-sensitive, plain list must use name order task-table columns reproduce row's primer (assembling params canonical column order part task-tables caller contract ). task_primer() normalises structure, meaning: hashes whatever params given. canonical, name-keyed representation caller contract assembled params built (task-tables, task-lists tables). Per three-step model RNG-consuming steps take primer task identity: sample – keyed (dataset, sim, replace) . nrow deliberately absent: every nrow shares one n_max-row draw fit step truncates inline (head(sample, nrow), RNG-free, separate primer), excluding nrow load-bearing sub-truncation property (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §5). fit – parent sample identity plus nrow fit-grid row (rescale, computable, at_boundary_ok, min_pmix name, range_shape1, range_shape2). nrow part fit primer: fit different truncation genuinely different computation. hc – parent fit identity plus hc-grid row (nboot, est_method, ci_method, parametric). ci scalar hc flag applied uniformly, part task identity, carried column rather primer field. Function-valued parameters (e.g. min_pmix) MUST referenced name, function value, recompile JIT move task's primer.
